task_completion.description: Verifies the creation of an alert named 'High_License_Usage_Warning' querying internal metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Alerts' page or a saved search configuration screen showing the name 'High_License_Usage_Warning' and a search string containing 'index=_internal' and 'kb'.

task_completion.description: Verifies the alert trigger condition is set to exceed 1000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the alert trigger conditions in the Splunk GUI showing a threshold configuration such as '> 1000' or 'is greater than 1000'.

task_completion.description: Verifies the creation of a dashboard named 'License_Consumption_Audit'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Dashboards list or an active dashboard view displaying the title 'License_Consumption_Audit'.

task_completion.description: Verifies the dashboard includes a visualization for volume by index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dashboard panel (chart or table) showing volume by index. Evidence includes the search string containing 'group="per_index_thruput"' and 'kb', or a chart clearly displaying index names on an axis/legend.

task_completion.description: Verifies the dashboard includes a visualization for volume by sourcetype.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second dashboard panel showing volume by sourcetype. Evidence includes the search string containing 'group="per_sourcetype_thruput"' and 'kb', or a chart clearly displaying sourcetypes on an axis/legend.

integrity.description: Ensures the agent used the Splunk Web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the Splunk Enterprise interface (http://localhost:8000) should be visible, rather than terminal commands executing API calls.

integrity.description: Ensures the visualizations and alerts are based on actual Splunk searches.
integrity.visual_evidence: Splunk UI elements like search bars, time pickers, and native Splunk charts/tables are visible, rather than mocked HTML pages or static text files.

integrity.description: Ensures the agent didn't bypass the UI by directly editing Splunk configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open with Splunk configuration files such as savedsearches.conf or dashboards.xml.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target alert must be named 'High_License_Usage_Warning'. The target dashboard must be named 'License_Consumption_Audit'. The Splunk web interface credentials are 'admin' and 'SplunkAdmin1!'.
